text:
Phase 0 should not merely “label receipt node ids.” It should introduce a **stable node label registry** per surface:

```text
status/<session>/working
status/<session>/title
status/<session>/activity
status/<session>/channels
status/<session>/ttl-arm
subscriptions/daemon/channels
subscriptions/daemon/addressed-pubkeys
subscriptions/session/<session>/channels
hook/<session>/cursor
hook/<session>/presence
hook/<session>/messages
```

Persist both id and label:

```json
{
  "inputs": [
    {"id": 12, "label": "status/s1/activity"}
  ],
  "derived": [
    {"id": 18, "label": "status/s1/content"}
  ],
  "collections": [
    {"id": 19, "label": "status/s1/coll"}
  ]
}
``` [...] ### 4.2 Stable node-label registry (adopt fully — Phase 0)

The shipped `changed_summary` persists graph-local integer ids (`{"inputs":[2,3]}`, `instrument.rs:112`) — useless to a human or agent a day later. Build a per-surface `NodeLabels{ by_id: BTreeMap<NodeId, String> }` populated at node creation (the reconcilers already name every node: `"activity"`, `"daemon-channels"`, `hook`'s six inputs are labeled at `hook_context/mod.rs:180-187`). Labels are stable, semantic paths: `status/<session>/activity`, `subscriptions/session/<session>/channels`, `hook/<session>/cursor`. Persist **both** id and label in the commits ledger and receipts v2. This is the precondition for every legibility claim — an agent cannot diagnose from `[2,3]`. [...] ```rust
pub struct NodeLabels { by_id: BTreeMap<NodeId, String> }   // Default + Clone
  fn new()
  fn record(&mut self, id: NodeId, label: impl Into<String>)   // called AT node creation
  fn label_of(&self, id: NodeId) -> Option<&str>
  fn labels_for(&self, ids: &[NodeId]) -> Vec<String>          // unregistered → "node:<n>"
  fn len(&self) / is_empty(&self) / iter() -> impl Iterator<(NodeId,&str)>
```
